dialogue_text: By means of the Golden Cap I shall command the Winged Monkeys to carry you to the gates of the Emerald City,
source_excerpt: "By means of the Golden Cap I shall command the Winged Monkeys to carry you to the gates of the Emerald City," said Glinda, "for it would be a shame to deprive the people of so wonderful a ruler."

dialogue_text: for it would be a shame to deprive the people of so wonderful a ruler.
source_excerpt: "By means of the Golden Cap I shall command the Winged Monkeys to carry you to the gates of the Emerald City," said Glinda, "for it would be a shame to deprive the people of so wonderful a ruler."
"Am I really wonderful?" asked the Scarecrow.

dialogue_text: What will become of you when Dorothy leaves this country?
source_excerpt: Turning to the Tin Woodman, she asked, "What will become of you when Dorothy leaves this country?"

dialogue_text: The Winkies were very kind to me, and wanted me to rule over them after the Wicked Witch died. I am fond of the Winkies, and if I could get back again to the Country of the West, I should like nothing better than to rule over them forever.

dialogue_text: My second command to the Winged Monkeys,
source_excerpt: "My second command to the Winged Monkeys," said Glinda "will be that they carry you safely to the land of the Winkies. Your brain may not be so large to look at as those of the Scarecrow, but you are really brighter than he is—when you a...

dialogue_text: will be that they carry you safely to the land of the Winkies. Your brain may not be so large to look at as those of the Scarecrow, but you are really brighter than he is—when you are well polished—and I am sure you will rule the Winkies wisely and well.

dialogue_text: When Dorothy has returned to her own home, what will become of you?
source_excerpt: Then the Witch looked at the big, shaggy Lion and asked, "When Dorothy has returned to her own home, what will become of you?"

dialogue_text: Over the hill of the Hammer-Heads,
source_excerpt: "Over the hill of the Hammer-Heads," he answered, "lies a grand old forest, and all the beasts that live there have made me their King. If I could only get back to this forest, I would pass my life very happily there."

dialogue_text: My third command to the Winged Monkeys,
source_excerpt: "My third command to the Winged Monkeys," said Glinda, "shall be to carry you to your forest. Then, having used up the powers of the Golden Cap, I shall give it to the King of the Monkeys, that he and his band may thereafter be free for...
